title: Independent Parallel Random Number Streams with dqrng
text: Parallel random number generation easy get wrong. Sharing one generator across threads data race, giving thread freshly built generator worse looks: seed every thread draws identical sequence, seed clock streams may overlap. robust approach seed single generator hand thread copy advanced substream. dqrng xoshiro256plus generator supports exactly long_jump(), skips far ahead substreams overlap. example draws uniform variates way.
code: . ├── DESCRIPTION                         # Package metadata ├── NAMESPACE                           # Function and dependency registration ├── R                                   # R functions │   ├── RcppExports.R                   # Autogenerated R to C++ bindings by Rcpp │   └── RcppRNGParallel-package.R       # Package documentation ├── README.md ├── man                                 # Package documentation │   ├── RcppRNGParallel-package.Rd │   ├── draw_numeric_unif.Rd │   └── max_cores.Rd ├── rngparallel.Rproj └── src                                 # Compiled code     ├── Makevars                        # Enable OpenMP     ├── Makevars.win     ├── RcppExports.cpp                 # Autogenerated R bindings     └── parallel_rng.cpp                # The parallel sampler using dqrng substreams

previous_headings: Generating Random Numbers in Parallel within C++
title: Independent Parallel Random Number Streams with dqrng
text: sampler lives src/parallel_rng.cpp. single dqrng::xoshiro256plus generator seeded host. Inside OpenMP parallel region, thread copies generator calls long_jump() count unique thread, landing substream thread visits. thread fills share output. source guards OpenMP directives #ifdef _OPENMP provides serial fallback, carries //' roxygen comments (trimmed excerpt ) Rcpp::compileAttributes() copies R/RcppExports.R. R:
code: // [[Rcpp::export]] std::vector<double> draw_numeric_unif(unsigned int n,                                       double min = 0.0, double max = 1.0,                                       int seed = 42, int n_cores = 1) {   std::vector<double> sample_values(n);   if (n_cores < 1) n_cores = 1;    // Base generator, seeded once on the host.   dqrng::xoshiro256plus base(seed);    #pragma omp parallel num_threads(n_cores)   {     // Each thread copies the base generator and jumps to an independent,     // non-overlapping substream so the threads never overlap.     dqrng::xoshiro256plus eng(base);     eng.long_jump(omp_get_thread_num() + 1);      dqrng::uniform_distribution dist(min, max);      #pragma omp for schedule(static)     for (unsigned int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {       sample_values[i] = dist(eng);     }   }    return sample_values; } draw_numeric_unif(100, n_cores = 2, seed = 42)

title: Independent Parallel Random Number Streams with dqrng
text: substreams deterministic given seed jump counts, result reproducible fixed seed n_cores within given build. schedule(static) clause fixes thread handles index, mapping index substream vary runs. thread occupies non-overlapping substream, threads never produce values, makes parallel draws statistically sound. Changing n_cores repartitions work, also changes values, OpenMP build serial fallback draw different streams design. corrects common mistake: handing thread default-constructed engine. leaves every thread seed, sampler simply repeat sequence every core.

code: LinkingTo:     Rcpp,     dqrng,     BH,     sitmo PKG_CXXFLAGS = $(SHLIB_OPENMP_CXXFLAGS) PKG_LIBS = $(SHLIB_OPENMP_CXXFLAGS)
